payload.input: But then Ethan moves, stepping forward with a sudden, desperate energy, placing himself between the photograph and their parents, his hands raised in surrender. "It’s not real," he blurts out, the lie tumbling out in a rush of words. "It’s edited. It’s... it’s a fake. We found it online, we were looking at it because... because we were trying to figure out if it was actually us, if someone was pretending to be us, because Jason showed me this site and there were people using our names, our pictures, and we were scared—"  "Shut up," their father roars, the sound shaking the windows, but Maya seizes the thread, her survival instinct kicking in through the haze of terror.
